title: Close hardware-gated caveats — real 910B3 runs (remote_bench wiring, real T_measured, bishengir chunk_kda reproduction)
context: User set up the remote 910B3 (ssh host '910B3' = 173.147.1.2, root, via local SOCKS proxy). CANN 9.0.0 at /usr/local/Ascend/ascend-toolkit (bishengir-compile + msprof at /usr/local/Ascend/cann-9.0.0/bin). Triton deps in conda env 'triton_hxl'. 8x 910B3 NPUs (chip0 in Alarm; chips1-7 OK). No remote repo yet. remote_bench.py hardcodes CANN '/usr/local/Ascend/cann/set_env.sh' + conda 'tlx' + has a literal '{{ }}' brace bug that breaks the shell '||' fallbacks. Goal: close the three caveats flagged on A.5/A.6/A.7 that were 'hardware-gated, never run on real hardware'.

stories.title: Fix remote_bench.py to match the real 910B3 environment
stories.description: Make the remote preamble match the actual machine: CANN set_env at /usr/local/Ascend/ascend-toolkit/set_env.sh, conda env 'triton_hxl', bishengir-compile on PATH (cann-9.0.0/bin). Fix the literal '{{ }}' double-brace shell bug to single-brace groups. Make CANN setenv path + conda env name overridable via env vars so the script is not brittle.
acceptanceCriteria: _REMOTE_PREAMBLE (or its replacement) sources /usr/local/Ascend/ascend-toolkit/set_env.sh by default and activates conda env 'triton_hxl' by default
CANN setenv path is overridable via VTRITON_REMOTE_CANN_SETENV and conda env via VTRITON_REMOTE_CONDA_ENV (defaults match the real machine)
All shell-group fallbacks use single-brace '{ cmd; }' form (no literal '{{ }}'), so the generated SSH command is valid bash
recompile_remote default bishengir path resolves to 'bishengir-compile' on PATH (after CANN sourcing), not the non-existent thirdparty/AscendNPU-IR build path
A unit test asserts the generated preamble string contains 'ascend-toolkit/set_env.sh' and 'conda activate triton_hxl' and contains no '{{'
python3 -m pytest tests/perfbound/ -q stays green (no regressions vs prior baseline)

stories.title: Establish remote config + connectivity, sync repo, smoke the env preamble on hardware
stories.description: Write ~/.vtriton_remote (host=910B3, path=~/vTriton). Sync the repo to the remote. Run the exact env preamble over SSH and confirm CANN sources, conda triton_hxl activates, and bishengir-compile + msprof + npu-smi are all reachable inside that environment.
acceptanceCriteria: ~/.vtriton_remote exists with [remote] host=910B3 and path pointing at the remote checkout
resolve_remote_config() returns host '910B3' with no CLI/env override (reads the config file)
Repo synced to remote ~/vTriton (rsync) — scripts/, perfbound/, test/, configs/ present remotely
Running the generated preamble over SSH succeeds: 'echo OK' returns OK with CANN+conda active
Inside the preamble env on remote: bishengir-compile --version (or --help) runs, msprof --version runs, npu-smi info lists 910B3 — captured as evidence in progress.txt

stories.title: Produce a REAL T_measured on the 910B3 and flow it through the three-level report
stories.description: Run a kernel that actually compiles+runs on the 910B3 under msprof, fetch op_summary CSV, parse T_measured via msprof_parser, and render the three-level reachability hierarchy (HIVM/DSL/measured) with a real author_headroom number. Closes the 'author_headroom never populated with real data' caveat.
acceptanceCriteria: A kernel is profiled on real hardware via remote_bench (msprof op_summary_*.csv fetched back to local)
parse_kernel_time_us() parses the real CSV to a finite positive T_measured (n_invocations >= 1 after warmup discard)
The kernel's bound (T_bound_HIVM/T_bound_DSL) is computed and the three-level report renders with real T_measured and a real author_headroom_us (or a labelled BOUND_VIOLATION if T_bound > T_measured)
The validation soundness check classifies the run as PASS or BOUND_VIOLATION (not EXECUTION_ERROR), with the real CSV path recorded as msprof_source
The real CSV + report output are saved under .omc/research/ as evidence, and progress.txt records the measured numbers

stories.title: Reproduce/resolve the bishengir chunk_kda compile on real CANN 9.0.0 and settle the xfail
stories.description: Run bishengir-compile on the chunk_kda kernel path on the real 910B3 (CANN 9.0.0). Record the definitive outcome: does it still crash in ConvertLinalgRToBinary (SmallVector assertion)? Does the HIVM/DES dump survive the crash so the modelling path still works even if codegen fails? Update tests/perfbound/test_chunk_kda_milestone.py xfail to reflect the evidence (keep xfail with concrete reason+log, or flip to pass if the bug is gone on this CANN).
acceptanceCriteria: bishengir-compile is run against the chunk_kda kernel on the remote 910B3 and the full stdout/stderr is captured to .omc/research/ as evidence
The outcome is definitively classified: (a) still crashes in ConvertLinalgRToBinary, (b) compiles successfully, or (c) different failure — with the exact error recorded
Whether the HIVM/DES graph dump is produced before the crash is determined (the TestDumpBeforeCodegen spike question is answered with evidence)
test_chunk_kda_milestone.py is updated so its xfail/skip condition matches reality on this CANN (reason string cites the captured evidence; flips to a real pass only if the kernel actually compiles)
python3 -m pytest tests/perfbound/test_chunk_kda_milestone.py -q reflects the real state (xfail with documented reason, or pass)

stories.title: Update PROGRESS.md + caveat docs to reflect the real hardware results
stories.description: Replace the 'hardware-gated / never run on real hardware' caveats with the evidence-backed outcomes from US-HW-003 and US-HW-004. Update .omc/plans/a6_progress.md blocker table and PROGRESS.md current-state section.
acceptanceCriteria: PROGRESS.md current-state section records: remote 910B3 wired, a real T_measured produced (with the number), and the bishengir chunk_kda outcome (resolved/still-blocked with evidence link)
.omc/plans/a6_progress.md blocker table updated: each previously hardware-gated row now points to the captured evidence and its resolved status
Any caveat that is genuinely closed is stated plainly; any that remains (e.g. a third-party compiler bug we cannot patch) is stated with the evidence and why it is out of our control
No overclaiming: docs distinguish 'ran on hardware and measured' from 'still blocked by external compiler bug'
